blocks.content: Seventh grade had lockers, which sixth grade hadn't. Your locker was your address, and that turned out to matter in ways it hadn't before.
Kit's locker was in the B-wing. Daisy's was in C. Eli had somehow already found both of them before homeroom.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Eleven minutes is enough. There's a new kid from Atlanta in homeroom 7B, the vending machine by the gym has been restocked, and I've already identified three people running for class president who are not going to win.
Savannah arrived with the binder system, color-coded, exactly as promised. She looked at the hallway the way she looked at most things — like she was quietly filing it away.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: Marcus says eighth grade is completely different.
The bell rang. Kit and Daisy went left toward homeroom 7A, Eli right toward 7B.
